lightBloom.note: Arc reactor is THE hero light source — needs aggressive bloom that spills onto adjacent biometric cards
Traveling data particles should leave bloom trails

lensFlare.note: Persistent anamorphic streak from arc reactor — this IS the Iron Man signature
As wireframe rotates, catch occasional hexagonal lens flare from bright vertices

chromaticAberration.note: 1.5px offset is present but should be DYNAMIC — increase to 3px during glitch bursts, snap back to 1.5px. Also add separate R/G/B channel offsets: R(+1,0), G(0,0), B(-1,+1)

filmGrain.note: 0.015 is too subtle for holographic projection. Increase to 0.03 and make it color grain (not monochrome) — RGB noise channels slightly offset

depthOfField.note: Present but underspecified. Depth layers 2-3 (bottom bar, floating cards) should have 2-3px Gaussian blur. Particles at depth 4 should have 4-6px bokeh blur

lightLeaks.note: Holographic projection light leak — cyan wash bleeds into corner

reflectionGlare.note: Faint upward-pointing trapezoid of light from the bottom — as if the hologram is being projected from below

particleEffects.note: 60 ambient particles exist but need SIZE VARIATION with distance. Add 5 very close particles (size 5-8px, blurred) that drift across foreground — parallax depth cue

cameraDrift.note: More drift than NASA — this is Tony Stark waving his hands, the 'camera' follows subtly

rackFocus.note: When token exhaustion threat rises to 7, rack focus to the threat panel
Then focus shifts to the incoming notification

dramaticReveals.note: Glitch reveal is specified but needs: RGB split (3px) -> recombine -> slight overshoot scale (102%) -> settle to 100%. Duration should be 20 frames, not instant
Cards should slide in one by one with slight overshoot bounce

breathingAnimations.note: HUD frame should breathe — alive, not static
Panel borders subtly brighten and dim

transitionMoments.description: Threat escalation sequence — threat panel severity 7 turns amber, rack focus, notification slides in, brief chromatic aberration spike to 4px, ambient particles speed up 2x for 20 frames

dataDensityGaps.fix: Add a small system clock with date: '2026.068 // 14:27:33 UTC' in dimCyan, plus uptime counter
Add a 5th row: 'LATENCY SPIKE' at severity 4, trend 'rising'. Also add percentage labels on severity bars
Add per-agent model name (e.g., 'opus-4') and session ID as tiny text below status badge
Add floating text annotations near vertices: 'ACTIVE', '4 SESSIONS', '2847 msg/s' — like JARVIS labels components in mid-air
The hex grid at 0.03 opacity is good. Add coordinate labels at hex intersections every 5th hex: 'A7', 'B3', etc. — military HUD grid reference

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Cool cyan-dominant with warm orange as danger accent — like Deakins lighting through cyan gels. Shadows are pure black (correct for hologram). Add very subtle blue (#001122) to the darkest areas instead of pure #000000

keyMoments.shift: Ambient hex grid shifts from cyan to warm orange tint for the right-panel area — localized color contamination
Entire frame desaturates 20% for 3 frames during glitch — momentary hologram instability

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: Orange (#ff6600) is only used for agent-gamma WARNING. Underutilized. Add orange accent lines to threat panel borders and high-severity notification card edges
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Pure black is correct for hologram but add a very faint radial gradient: center #000811 edges #000000 — suggests depth behind the projection

typographyNotes.fonts: Rajdhani + Share Tech Mono is an excellent pairing. Rajdhani gives the futuristic UI feel, Share Tech Mono grounds the data
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: Share Tech Mono is good but consider Oxanium for labels — it has a more angular, holographic quality
typographyNotes.displayFont: The title at 28px Rajdhani 700 could use letter-spacing: 8px as specified, but also add text-transform: uppercase if not already
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 10px labels are too small. Bump to 11px. The 13px readout is fine. Add a 'mega' tier at 48px for the arc reactor percentage display — it deserves hero treatment

continuousAmbience.sound: Low harmonic hum that modulates with rotation speed — the hologram projector
Deep 60Hz electrical hum with subtle harmonic overtones on each ring pulse
Very faint high-frequency shimmer — holographic air distortion

eventSounds.sound: Sharp glass tap + short whoosh as each card arrives
Soft synthetic swipe — rising pitch for upward arcs, falling for downward
Digital distortion crackle — 0.1s burst of bit-crushed noise

silenceMoments.description: Complete silence before hud-title glitch reveal — then the system powers on with a rising tone
